748→```bash 749→erk exec track-learn-result \ 750→ --issue <parent-issue-number> \ 751→ --status completed_with_plan \ 752→ --learn-plan <learn-pr-number> 753→``` 754→ 755→This sets `learn_status: completed_with_plan` and `learn_plan_issue: <N>` on the parent plan, 756→enabling the TUI to show the linked learn plan. 757→

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### get-plans-for-objective
Fetch erk-plans linked to an objective.
**Usage:** `erk exec get-plans-for-objective` <objective_number>

### get-prs-for-objective
Fetch erk-prs linked to an objective.
**Usage:** `erk exec get-prs-for-objective` <objective_number>

13→- **[checkout-helpers.md](checkout-helpers.md)** — writing checkout commands, creating worktrees in checkout commands, implementing branch checkout logic 14→- **[checkout-teleport-split.md](checkout-teleport-split.md)** — working with erk pr checkout or erk pr teleport commands, understanding the difference between checkout and teleport, modifying cmux-open-pr command 15→- **[checkout-three-path-logic.md](checkout-three-path-logic.md)** — modifying erk br co, working with --for-plan flag, changing slot allocation behavior 16→- **[ci-aware-commands.md](ci-aware-commands.md)** — implementing commands that behave differently in CI, checking if code is running in GitHub Actions, skipping user-interactive steps in automated environments 17→- **[cli-options-validation.md](cli-options-validation.md)** — adding new CLI options or flags, implementing option validation logic, encountering unvalidated user input

1→--- 2→title: Checkout Three-Path Logic 3→category: cli 4→read_when: 5→ - "modifying erk br co" 6→ - "working with --for-plan flag" 7→ - "changing slot allocation behavior" 8→last_audited: "2026-03-05 00:00 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# Checkout Three-Path Logic 13→ 14→`erk br co` uses a three-path decision tree when `--for-plan` is involved, implemented in `branch/checkout_cmd.py:614-717`. 15→ 16→## Decision Tree 17→ 18→### Path 1: Stack in Place (`current_assignment is not None`) 19→ 20→The user is in a slot-assigned worktree and the branch has a current assignment. 21→ 22→- Updates assignment to new tip 23→- Calls `_rebase_and_track_for_plan()` if setup exists 24→- Calls `_setup_impl_for_plan()` if setup exists 25→- Calls `_perform_checkout()` with `force_script_activation=True` 26→- Returns early 27→ 28→### Path 2: Checkout in Current Worktree (`setup is not None and not new_slot`) 29→ 30→The user passed `--for-plan` (so `setup is not None`) but did NOT pass `--new-slot`.
... [35 lines omitted] ...
The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/checkout-three-path-logic.md has been updated successfully.

The `--learn-plan` in the "New Name" column means it was renamed TO `--learn-plan` previously. Now we're renaming it again to `--learn-pr`. I should update this table to show the current state:
session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md has been updated successfully.

22→Plan metadata (`plan-header` block) intentionally omits `branch_name` at creation time because of a temporal gap in the plan lifecycle: 23→ 24→1. **Plan saved** → GitHub issue created (no branch exists yet) 25→2. **Branch created** → User runs `erk br co --for-plan {issue}` (branch now exists) 26→3. **Implementation starts** → `impl-signal started` writes `branch_name` into the plan-header 27→ 28→The gap between steps 1 and 2 is unavoidable: the plan must exist before the branch can be named after it (the branch name contains the issue number). Attempting to set `branch_name` during save would require either predicting the branch name (fragile — the user might choose a custom name) or creating the branch immediately (wrong — the user hasn't started work yet). 29→ 30→## Two-Layer Resolution 31→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: 2. **Branch created** → User runs `erk br co --for-plan {issue}` (branch now exists)
2. **Branch created** → User runs `erk br co --for-pr {issue}` (branch now exists)

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md has been updated successfully.

50→Before rebasing, the function follows the LBYL pattern: it checks `list_local_branches()` for the parent, and only if absent, fetches from origin and creates a tracking branch. This avoids unnecessary network calls when the branch already exists locally. 51→ 52→## Integration Points 53→ 54→The function is called at three points in `erk br co --for-plan`: 55→ 56→| Context | When | 57→| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- | 58→| Stack-in-place | Current worktree already assigned to pool slot | 59→| Single worktree match | Exactly one worktree contains the branch | 60→| Multi-worktree selection | Multiple worktrees, one has direct checkout | 61→ 62→All three follow the same sequence: `_rebase_and_track_for_plan()` -> `_setup_impl_for_plan()` -> `_perform_checkout()`. 63→ 64→## Mirror Pattern
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: The function is called at three points in `erk br co --for-plan`:
The function is called at three points in `erk br co --for-pr`:

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/stacked-plan-branch-rebase.md has been updated successfully.

26→ 27→Each plan implementation runs in an isolated worktree: 28→ 29→1. `erk br co --for-plan <issue-number>` creates a new worktree from the plan 30→2. The worktree gets its own `.erk/impl-context/` folder with the plan content 31→3. Implementation happens entirely within the worktree 32→4. After PR lands, the worktree is cleaned up 33→ 34→### Why Isolation? 35→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: 1. `erk br co --for-plan <issue-number>` creates a new worktree from the plan
1. `erk br co --for-pr <issue-number>` creates a new worktree from the plan

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/plan-execution-patterns.md has been updated successfully.

32→## Shell Activation Pattern 33→ 34→The `new_wt` variants use the `source "$(erk ... --script)"` pattern because they create a new worktree and need to navigate the shell there. This is required because subprocess directory changes don't persist to the calling shell. 35→ 36→The `current_wt` variants use a simple `&&` chain (`erk br co --for-plan N && erk implement`) since the user is already in the correct directory — no shell activation is needed. 37→ 38→See [Shell Activation Pattern](../cli/shell-activation-pattern.md) for the full explanation. 39→ 40→## Format Functions 41→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: The `current_wt` variants use a simple `&&` chain (`erk br co --for-plan N && erk implement`) since the user is already in the correct directory — no shell activation is needed.
The `current_wt` variants use a simple `&&` chain (`erk br co --for-pr N && erk implement`) since the user is already in the correct directory — no shell activation is needed.

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/next-steps-output.md has been updated successfully.

158→```bash 159→erk exec track-learn-result \ 160→ --issue <parent-issue-number> \ 161→ --status completed_with_plan \ 162→ --learn-plan <learn-pr-number> 163→``` 164→ 165→This sets `learn_status` and `learn_plan_issue` on the parent plan. 166→ 167→If no documentation was needed: 168→ 169→```bash 170→erk exec track-learn-result \ 171→ --issue <parent-issue-number> \ 172→ --status completed_no_plan

83→| ------------------------ | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | 84→| `/erk:pr-submit` | `erk pr submit` | Agent delegates PR submission from current branch to CLI | 85→| `/erk:pr-address-remote` | `erk launch pr-address --pr <pr>` | Agent finds PR in conversation, CLI triggers GitHub workflow | 86→| `/erk:prepare` | `erk br co --for-plan <issue>` | Agent finds issue in conversation, CLI creates worktree | 87→ 88→**Pattern characteristics:** 89→ 90→- Agent commands are thin wrappers that extract context

| `/erk:prepare` | `erk br co --for-pr <issue>` | Agent finds issue in conversation, CLI creates worktree |

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/command-boundaries.md has been updated successfully.

645→### Plan Folder 646→ 647→A `.erk/impl-context/` folder containing implementation plans and progress tracking for a feature. 648→ 649→**Usage**: `erk create --from-plan-file my-plan.md my-feature` 650→ 651→**Behavior**: 652→ 653→- Plan file is converted to `.erk/impl-context/` folder structure in the new worktree 654→- Contains two files: 655→ - `plan.md` - Immutable implementation plan 656→ - `progress.md` - Mutable progress tracking with checkboxes 657→- `.erk/impl-context/` is gitignored (not committed) 658→- Useful for keeping implementation notes with the working code 659→ 660→**Benefits**: 661→ 662→- Separation of concerns: plan content vs progress tracking 663→- No risk of corrupting plan while updating progress 664→- Progress visible in `erk status` output 665→ 666→**Example**: 667→ 668→```bash 669→# Create plan 670→echo "## Implementation Plan\n1. Step 1\n2. Step 2" > plan.md 671→ 672→# Create worktree from plan file 673→erk create --from-plan-file plan.md my-feature 674→
... [10 lines omitted] ...
The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/glossary.md has been updated successfully.
The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/glossary.md has been updated successfully.

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me carefully analyze this conversation to create a comprehensive summary.
## The Task The user ran `/erk:plan-implement 9310` which triggered the implementation of plan #9310 - renaming CLI flags from "plan" to "pr" across the erk codebase (Objective #9109, Node 8.2).
## What Was Done
### Setup - Ran `erk exec setup-impl --issue 9310` which set up the implementation directory - Read the plan from `.erk/impl-context/plnd--O9109-rename-cli-flags-pla-03-20-2357/plan.md`
### The Plan 6 groups of renames: 1. `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` in branch/checkout_cmd.py, branch/create_cmd.py, next_steps.py, plan_detail_screen.py 2. `--from-plan` → `--from-pr`, `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file`, `--keep-plan-file` → `--keep-pr-file`, `--copy-plan` → `--copy-pr` in wt/create_cmd.py 3. `--plan` → `--pr` in duplicate_check_cmd.py, objective_fetch_context.py 4. `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` in track_learn_result.py 5. `get_plans_for_objective` → `get_prs_for_objective` (file rename + registration) 6. Fix stale `--plan` → `--pr` in objective_helpers.py
### Implementation Progress
**Phase 1 (completed)**: next_steps.py was already updated (no changes needed)
**Phase 2 (completed)**: CLI source files - Used a `libcst-refactor` subagent to update: - `checkout_cmd.py`: `--for-plan`/`for_plan` → `--for-pr`/`for_pr` - `create_cmd.py` (branch): `--for-plan`/`for_plan` → `--for-pr`/`for_pr` - `duplicate_check_cmd.py`: `--plan`/`plan` → `--pr`/`pr` - `objective_fetch_context.py`: `--plan` → `--pr` (but then discovered this caused a conflict with an existing `--pr` option, so reverted) - `track_learn_result.py`: `--learn-plan`/`learn_plan` → `--learn-pr`/`learn_pr`, error codes updated - `objective_helpers.py`: `--plan {pr_number}` → `--pr {pr_number}` - Used `sed` to update wt/create_cmd.py for 4 flag renames (but ran into issues with multi-command sed syntax)
**Key issue discovered**: In `objective_fetch_context.py`, the `--plan` option at line 153 was renamed to `--pr`, but there was ALREADY a `--pr` option at line 136. This would create a duplicate Click option. The fix was to revert `objective_fetch_context.py` to its original state (keeping `--plan`).
**Phase 3 (completed)**: Exec command rename - Created new file `get_prs_for_objective.py` - Updated `group.py` import and registration - Deleted old `get_plans_for_objective.py` via `git rm`
**Phase 4 (completed)**: TUI - updated `plan_detail_screen.py`'s 5 hardcoded `--for-plan` strings to `--for-pr`
**Phase 5 (completed)**: Tests - Created new `test_get_prs_for_objective.py`, deleted old `test_get_plans_for_objective.py` - Updated multiple test files via subagent - Manually fixed `test_track_learn_result.py` (was accidentally wiped by bad sed command, restored from git, then made targeted edits) - Updated `test_duplicate_check.py`, `test_remote_paths.py`, `test_create_copy_impl.py` - Reverted bad changes to `test_objective_fetch_context.py`
**Phase 6 (in progress at summary time)**: Docs/skills - Updated `.claude/commands/erk/learn.md`: `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` - Updated `.claude/commands/local/objective-view.md`: `get-plans-for-objective` → `get-prs-for-objective` - Updated `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md`: table entries and section headers - Updated multiple `docs/learned/` files
**Phase 7 (pending)**: Regenerate exec reference docs via `erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs` **Phase 8 (pending)**: Run CI checks **Phase 9 (pending)**: Submit PR
## Errors
1. **sed command wiped test_track_learn_result.py**: Using multiple sed commands in sequence with bad shell syntax wiped the file. Fixed by restoring from git and making targeted Edit tool changes.
2. **objective_fetch_context.py duplicate --pr conflict**: The plan said to rename `--plan` to `--pr`, but there was already a `--pr` option in the file. Fixed by reverting the change to keep `--plan` as-is.
3. **test_objective_fetch_context.py bad change**: Made a change that broke the test (changed `--plan` to `--pr` in CLI invocation list which would create duplicate `--pr` args). Fixed by reverting.
## Current state at summary time The conversation was cut off during Phase 7 (regenerating exec reference docs), which comes after Phase 6 was just completed. The summary was requested right as Phase 7 was starting.
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user ran `/erk:plan-implement 9310` to implement plan #9310: "Rename CLI flags from 'plan' to 'pr' (Objective #9109, Node 8.2)". This is the final node of a larger rename initiative renaming "plan" terminology to "pr" across all APIs. The task involves 6 groups of mechanical CLI flag renames across source files, tests, docs, and skills, with no backwards compatibility needed.
2. Key Technical Concepts: - **Click CLI framework** — Python CLI options defined with `@click.option("--flag-name", "python_param_name", ...)` where both the flag string and Python parameter name need renaming - **erk workspace/worktree management** — erk is a development workflow tool with commands for branch management, worktree creation, and plan tracking - **Plan/PR lifecycle** — "plans" are draft PRs with `erk-pr` label in GitHub, now being fully renamed to "pr" terminology - **exec command registry** — `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py` registers all exec subcommands via `add_command()` - **git rm** — used to properly delete/rename files in the git index - **erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs** — tool to regenerate the `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` auto-generated docs - **TodoWrite tool** — used to track implementation phases
3. Files and Code Sections:
**Source Files Modified:**
- `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py` - `--for-plan`/`for_plan` → `--for-pr`/`for_pr` in option, params, error messages, docstrings, comments - Internal private functions `_setup_impl_for_plan` and `_rebase_and_track_for_plan` kept as-is (not CLI flags)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py` - `--for-plan`/`for_plan` → `--for-pr`/`for_pr`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/create_cmd.py` - `--from-plan-file`/`from_plan_file` → `--from-pr-file`/`from_pr_file` - `--keep-plan-file`/`keep_plan_file` → `--keep-pr-file`/`keep_pr_file` - `--from-plan`/`from_plan` → `--from-pr`/`from_pr` - `--copy-plan`/`copy_plan` → `--copy-pr`/`copy_pr`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py` - `--plan`/`plan` → `--pr`/`pr` (kept `-p` short alias)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py` - **KEPT AS-IS** — original had `--plan` at line 153 (param `pr_number_arg`) but there's already a `--pr` option at line 136 (param `pr_number`). Renaming `--plan` to `--pr` would create duplicate Click options, so this file was reverted.
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_result.py` - `--learn-plan`/`learn_plan` → `--learn-pr`/`learn_pr` - Error codes: `missing-learn-plan` → `missing-learn-pr`, `unexpected-learn-plan` → `unexpected-learn-pr` - Data field names `learn_plan_issue` and `learn_plan_pr` kept as-is (not CLI flags)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_plans_for_objective.py` → **DELETED** (via `git rm`)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_prs_for_objective.py` → **CREATED NEW** ```python @click.command(name="get-prs-for-objective") @click.argument("objective_number", type=int) @click.pass_context def get_prs_for_objective(ctx: click.Context, objective_number: int) -> None: """Fetch erk-prs linked to an objective.""" ```
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py` - Import changed: `from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.get_plans_for_objective import get_plans_for_objective` → `from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.get_prs_for_objective import get_prs_for_objective` - Registration: `exec_group.add_command(get_plans_for_objective, name="get-plans-for-objective")` → `exec_group.add_command(get_prs_for_objective, name="get-prs-for-objective")`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/objective_helpers.py` - Line 91: `f"/erk:objective-update-with-closed-plan --plan {pr_number} --objective {objective}"` → `--pr {pr_number}`
- `src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py` - 5 hardcoded strings: `erk br co --for-plan {N}` → `erk br co --for-pr {N}` (lines 362, 368, 680, 686, 993)
**Test Files Modified:**
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_get_plans_for_objective.py` → **DELETED** (via `git rm`)
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_get_prs_for_objective.py` → **CREATED NEW** - All test functions renamed from `test_get_plans_for_objective_*` to `test_get_prs_for_objective_*` - Import updated to use `get_prs_for_objective`
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_track_learn_result.py` - `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` in CLI invocations - Error message assertions updated: `"learn-plan is required"` → `"learn-pr is required"`, `"learn-plan should not be provided"` → `"learn-pr should not be provided"`
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/branch/test_create_cmd.py` - `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` in CLI invocations and assertions
- `tests/commands/branch/test_checkout_cmd.py` - `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` in CLI invocations and assertions
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_plan_file_operations.py` - `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file`, `--keep-plan-file` → `--keep-pr-file` - Error assertions updated
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_output_modes.py` - `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file`
- `tests/commands/test_create_copy_impl.py` - `--copy-plan` → `--copy-pr`, `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file` - Assert updated: `"--copy-plan"` → `"--copy-pr"` in result.output check
- `tests/commands/management/test_impl.py` - `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file`
- `tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py` - `"erk br co --for-plan 123"` → `"erk br co --for-pr 123"` assertions
- `tests/tui/app/test_plan_detail_screen.py` - `"erk br co --for-plan 123"` → `"erk br co --for-pr 123"` assertion
- `tests/commands/pr/test_duplicate_check.py` - `--plan` → `--pr` in CLI invocations
- `tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` - `--plan` → `--pr` in CLI invocations
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_fetch_context.py` - **KEPT AS-IS** — kept `--plan` to match the source file which also keeps `--plan` (due to duplicate `--pr` conflict)
**Docs/Skills Modified:**
- `.claude/commands/erk/learn.md`: `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` (line ~752) - `.claude/commands/local/objective-view.md`: `get-plans-for-objective` → `get-prs-for-objective` - `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md`: - Table entry: `get-plans-for-objective` → `get-prs-for-objective` - Section header and usage: `### get-plans-for-objective` → `### get-prs-for-objective` - Flag table: `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` - `docs/learned/cli/index.md`: `--for-plan flag` → `--for-pr flag` - `docs/learned/cli/checkout-three-path-logic.md`: multiple `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md`: migration table updated - `docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md`: `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/planning/stacked-plan-branch-rebase.md`: `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/planning/plan-execution-patterns.md`: `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/planning/next-steps-output.md`: `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md`: `--learn-plan` → `--learn-pr` - `docs/learned/architecture/command-boundaries.md`: `--for-plan` → `--for-pr` - `docs/learned/glossary.md`: `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file` (2 occurrences)
4. Errors and fixes:
- **sed command accidentally wiped `test_track_learn_result.py`**: When running multiple sed commands in a single shell line using semicolons, the shell interpreted them incorrectly and wiped the file. Fixed by running `git checkout HEAD -- tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_track_learn_result.py` to restore from git, then using targeted `Edit` tool calls instead.
- **Duplicate `--pr` option in `objective_fetch_context.py`**: The plan specified renaming `--plan` (line 153) to `--pr`, but the file already had a `--pr` option at line 136 (for a different purpose: the implementation PR number). Creating two `--pr` options in the same Click command would be invalid. Fix: reverted `objective_fetch_context.py` to its original state via `git checkout HEAD -- src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py`, keeping `--plan` as-is. Also reverted the corresponding bad change in `test_objective_fetch_context.py`.
- **Bad test edit in `test_objective_fetch_context.py`**: The test invocation `["--pr", "6517", "--objective", "6423", "--pr", "6513"]` was wrong (duplicate `--pr`). Fixed by reverting with Edit tool to restore `["--pr", "6517", "--objective", "6423", "--plan", "6513"]`.
5. Problem Solving: - The plan specified 6 groups of renames, implemented in order per dependencies - Phase 1 was trivially done (next_steps.py was already updated in a prior node) - The objective_fetch_context.py skip was an important architectural decision: the plan's Group 3 included this file, but renaming `--plan` to `--pr` there would break the CLI with duplicate option names. The `--plan` option serves a different purpose (direct plan lookup by number) vs the existing `--pr` (the implementation PR being tracked). Keeping `--plan` is the correct choice. - All CLI invocations in tests were systematically found via grep and updated
6. All user messages: - The user's only message was the slash command: `/erk:plan-implement 9310` with the full command definition shown
7. Pending Tasks: - **Phase 7**: Regenerate exec reference docs via `erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs` (was about to start) - **Phase 8**: Run CI checks (ruff, ty, pytest for targeted test suites, full grep verification) - **Phase 9**: Signal GitHub ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, run CI, submit PR via `erk pr submit` - **impl-signal started** was attempted but failed (session ID not available in this context)
8. Current Work: Phase 6 (Docs/skills updates) was just completed. The last edit made was to `docs/learned/glossary.md` updating `--from-plan-file` to `--from-pr-file`. A final grep confirmed no remaining stale references in docs/skills: ``` grep -rn "\-\-for-plan\|\-\-from-plan\|\-\-copy-plan\|\-\-keep-plan\|\-\-learn-plan\|get-plans-for-objective..." returned no results ``` Phase 7 was starting next: running `erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs` to regenerate the exec reference documentation.
9. Optional Next Step: The next step is **Phase 7**: Regenerate exec reference docs. The plan states: > "7. **Regenerate exec reference docs** via `erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs`"
This command auto-generates `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` from the actual CLI commands. Since the `get-plans-for-objective` command was renamed to `get-prs-for-objective` and `--learn-plan` was renamed to `--learn-pr`, the reference docs need to be regenerated to reflect these changes. After that, Phase 8 (CI checks) and Phase 9 (PR submission) follow.. Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

tool_use.name: TodoWrite
tool_use.param: [{'content': 'Phase 7: Regenerate exec reference docs via erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs'...[truncated 160 chars]...'}, {'content': 'Phase 9: Submit PR', 'status': 'pending', 'activeForm': 'Submitting PR'}]

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs` and report the results. Working directory: /home/runner/work/erk/erk

session.tool_result:
Success: erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs passed
The command completed successfully with the following results:
- Generated `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` with documentation for 92 top-level commands - The file was formatted according to project standards - Dependencies were installed/verified (uv set up a virtual environment with 112 packages including erk-mcp, erk-dev, and supporting tools)
The reference documentation has been generated and is ready for use at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md`. agentId: a1efcad (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
